tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Open Google and search 'warm backpacking March US'
tasks.hook_line: 9 days out and no destination locked — everything else depends on this.
tasks.reward: Unlocks flight booking and gear planning for the whole trip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip the weekend of March 14-15, 2026. It's for my friend Mariano's 30th birthday — he wants something outdoorsy before we fly to Chicago for the party. I need somewhere in the US with temps above 60F, good for beginners, within 3-4 hours of major airports. Give me 3-5 options with pros/cons, difficulty level, permit requirements, and nearest airports.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Go to rei.com and search 'gear rental'
tasks.hook_line: First time renting gear from REI — they do full backpacking kits.
tasks.reward: Solves the gear problem without buying $800 worth of equipment
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out REI gear rentals for a 1-2 night backpacking trip March 14-15. I need a backpack, tent, and sleeping bag. I've never rented from REI before. Tell me what their rental process looks like, what's included in typical packages, pricing, how far in advance to reserve, and if there are any gotchas. Also confirm if their gear works for warm-weather trips (60-80F temps).

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights for March 13 departures
tasks.hook_line: 9 days out — prices only go up from here.
tasks.reward: Locks in the trip logistics and calms the planning anxiety
nuggets: Friday departure needed for weekend trip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book flights for a backpacking trip March 13-15, 2026. I'm flying from [your home city] to [destination TBD — will provide once decided]. I need outbound Friday March 13 (early enough to start hiking that afternoon) and return Sunday March 15 (late afternoon/evening). Show me how to search efficiently, what to look for in timing, and any tips for last-minute weekend bookings.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Search flights [backpacking city] to Chicago March 15
tasks.hook_line: Chicago flights already booked — this is the last piece of the travel puzzle.
tasks.reward: Completes the entire travel routing for Mariano's birthday
nuggets: Chicago has Midway and O'Hare options
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book a flight from [backpacking destination] to Chicago on Sunday March 15, 2026. I'm finishing a backpacking trip that day and need to get to Chicago for my friend's 30th birthday celebration. Looking for late afternoon or evening departure (after 3pm), arriving Chicago by 9-10pm. Show me the best options balancing price and timing. I already have a return flight from Chicago booked.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano asking what he actually wants
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone who invited you on a backpacking trip for their own birthday?
tasks.reward: Shows up with something thoughtful instead of scrambling last-minute
nuggets: 30th birthdays often want experiences over stuff
Backpacking gear = already covered by trip
Chicago food/drink gift card could work
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out a gift for my friend Mariano's 30th birthday. He's the one who organized a backpacking trip for his own birthday weekend, then we're going to Chicago for the actual celebration on March 19. He's into outdoors, fitness, good food, and travel. Budget flexible but not trying to go overboard. Should I even get a gift given I'm flying out for his birthday? If yes, what are some thoughtful options that aren't generic?

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: 4 pairs of glasses in Japan could save $400+ vs US prices.
tasks.reward: Gets the shopping done early so the rest of the trip is pure exploration
nuggets: JINS and Zoff = Japanese chains with English
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto where I can buy prescription glasses as an American tourist. I'm visiting March 25-27 and want to buy 4 pairs early in the trip. I need stores that: speak some English, accept foreign prescriptions, can make glasses quickly (ideally same-day), and have good prices. Give me 3-5 specific stores with addresses, Google Maps links, and what to expect for pricing and turnaround time.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Call insurance company and ask about international glasses
tasks.hook_line: Can you actually get insurance reimbursement for glasses bought overseas?
tasks.reward: Confirms you can buy with confidence and get reimbursed back home
nuggets: Most US vision plans reimburse foreign purchases
Prescription needs to be current within 1-2 years
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out what I need to buy prescription glasses in Japan and get reimbursed by US insurance. I have vision insurance through [your provider]. I want to buy 4 pairs during my March 23-April 7 trip. What documentation do I need from the Japanese store? Will my US prescription work? How do I submit for reimbursement when I get back? Are there any limits on number of pairs or annual maximums I should know about?

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks and less touristy spots. Front-load the eyeglasses shopping on day 1.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town break between temple cities — what's actually there?
tasks.reward: Locks in the mountain town itinerary for the trip's midpoint
nuggets: Hiking trails in Japanese Alps nearby
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Takayama, Japan (dates TBD, probably 1-2 days in late March/early April 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We're coming from Kyoto and going to Tokyo. We want a slower pace here — less temple-hopping, more nature and local culture. We like hiking, good food, sake, and charming towns. Give me a 1-2 day itinerary with specific recommendations, opening hours, and how to get around.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and create a 'Tokyo' saved list
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — how do you even begin to narrow it down?
tasks.reward: Finishes all activity planning for the entire Japan trip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Tokyo (dates TBD, probably 4-5 days in early April 2026). This is the final leg of a 2-week Japan trip with my girlfriend Dana. We'll be templed-out by this point. Focus on: food markets, neighborhoods to wander, cool shopping (watches, fountain pens, stationery), and a couple unique Tokyo experiences. Give me a loose day-by-day structure with specific neighborhoods, must-visit spots, and things to book in advance. Keep it flexible — not a minute-by-minute plan.

tasks.first_domino: Open VS Code and pull up the task-pilot project
tasks.hook_line: Extension skeleton is done — now it's about making it actually useful.
tasks.reward: Moves the project from concept to functional prototype
nuggets: VS Code API for editor integration
Need to track file changes in real-time
tasks.system_prompt: Help me continue building the VS Code AI Task Pilot extension. It's a tool to help with AI-assisted development while maintaining task focus. Core goal: visualize work in progress and provide AI with context of ongoing work while editing a shared document locally. Current state: basic extension structure exists. Next steps: implement file watching, task parsing from markdown, and basic UI panel. Walk me through the architecture decisions and code structure for these features.

tasks.first_domino: Sketch out what the task panel should look like
tasks.hook_line: How do you show task context without turning into another todo list?
tasks.reward: Defines the UX that makes this extension different from everything else
nuggets: VS Code webview panels for custom UI
Can use React in VS Code extensions
Status bar items for quick access
tasks.system_prompt: Help me design the UI for VS Code AI Task Pilot. It needs to show: current task context, work in progress, and recent changes — without becoming a cluttered todo list. Target user: ADHD developer who loses track of what they were doing. The UI should live in a VS Code panel, be glanceable, and help resume work quickly. Give me 2-3 design concepts with pros/cons, considering VS Code's UI patterns and webview capabilities.

tasks.first_domino: Define what 'context' means for this extension
tasks.hook_line: How do you give AI the right context without overwhelming it with noise?
tasks.reward: Solves the core problem that makes this extension valuable
nuggets: Export context as markdown for AI prompt
tasks.system_prompt: Help me design the AI context management system for VS Code AI Task Pilot. The goal: automatically gather relevant context from the workspace and format it for pasting into an AI conversation. Context should include: current task description, recent file changes, open files, and any notes. It should be smart about what's relevant (not just dump everything). Walk me through: what data to track, how to store it, how to format it for AI consumption, and how to keep it lightweight.

recommended_slots.reason: Most urgent with tight deadline (9 days) and blocks all other trip planning — perfect for ramp_up research energy
Low context cost research task that advances a major trip — good for morning energy outside work hours
Quick 20-min task with novelty hook — palette cleanser that complements slot 1 planning
